data.title: Constellation Energy in settlement talks with DoJ over Calpine deal - Bloomberg
data.description:
Constellation Energy (CEG) is working out a settlement with the U.S. Department of Justice that would pave the way for the closure of its $16.4 billion acquisition of Calpine Corp., Bloomberg reported Wednesday, in a deal that would create the largest U.S. power fleet.

Representatives of Constellation (CEG) and Calpine were set to meet with the head of the DoJ's antitrust division in early November to argue their case against a lawsuit, but the meeting reportedly was canceled after the companies made a stronger proposal - which included the sale of additional plants - to resolve concerns about competition in power markets.

The companies already had offered to divest four of Calpine's power plants in Pennsylvania and Delaware as part of a separate agreement with the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission that was reached in July, but the deal did not sufficiently ease the DoJ’s concerns, and the companies came back with an improved offer in recent weeks, according to the report.

Constellation (CEG) said in June it received approval for the deal from state regulators in Texas and New York; DoJ clearance is the last remaining hurdle before the companies can close the deal, which they have said they plan to do by the end of this year.

data.title: Medtronic gets FDA clearance of Hugo robotic-assisted surgery system
data.description:
• Medtronic (MDT) has received approval from the U.S. FDA for the Hugo robotic surgery system, which can now be used in urologic surgeries.
  • The Hugo RAS system is now  approved for minimally invasive urologic procedures such as prostatectomy, nephrectomy, and cystectomy.

data.title: Snowflake reveals collabs as Q3 results top estimates, but shares fall on in-line Q4 product revenue guidance
data.description:
Snowflake (SNOW) reported its third quarter fiscal 2026 financial results after the bell on Wednesday, which reflected a year-over-year revenue increase of 29%.

However, shares were down 8% during early post-market trading as its fourth-quarter product revenue guidance was barely above the consensus estimate.

For the quarter ended October 31, the cloud-based data management company reported adjusted earnings per share of $0.35 versus the consensus estimate of $0.31. It reported GAAP EPS of ($0.87) compared to the estimate of ($0.96).

Revenue for the third quarter increased 29% year over year to total $1.21B, which was more than the $1.18B estimate. Adjusted gross margin was also better than expected, coming in at 73% versus the 72% estimate. Free cash flow increased by 9% year over year to $113.6M. Remaining performance obligations surged 37% year over year to $7.88B. The company also posted a net retention rate of 125%.

Looking ahead, Snowflake projects fourth-quarter product revenue ranging from $1.195B to $1.2B, which is just above the $1.9B estimate.

For the entirety of fiscal 2026, Snowflake increased its product revenue guidance to $4.45B from $4.4B. The estimate calls for $4.41B.

"Snowflake is the cornerstone for our customers' data and AI strategies, driving real business impact at scale," said Snowflake CEO Sridhar Ramaswamy. "Snowflake Intelligence, our enterprise AI agent, saw the fastest adoption ramp in Snowflake history and is transforming how businesses interact with their data, delivering real-time, actionable intelligence. Combined with our strategic partnerships with the world’s leading AI model providers, clouds and application platforms, Snowflake is supercharging the entire data lifecycle with AI-driven capabilities."

Snowflake also announced expanded partnerships with Anthropic (ANTHRO), Accenture (ACN) and Amazon Web Services (AMZN).

The multi-year, $200M agreement with Anthropic will bring the AI startup's Claude models to the Snowflake platform. This next phase of collaboration is focused on deploying AI agents on Snowflake capable of handling multi-step analysis, powered by Claude's advanced reasoning capabilities and ability to securely handle sensitive data.

Accenture and Snowflake are collaborating to scale generative AI innovation and business outcomes for clients. They announced the formation of the Accenture Snowflake Business Group, created to help enterprises reinvent their business models by harnessing the power of cloud, AI and data.

Snowflake also announced new integrations with AWS as its sales surpassed $2B on the AWS Marketplace this year.

"Snowflake and AWS have built one of the strongest collaborations in the cloud industry today, helping organizations modernize faster and unlock new value from their data," said Mike Gannon, Chief Revenue Officer, Snowflake.

data.title: Equinix CFO Keith Taylor plans to retire
data.description:
• Equinix (EQIX) on Wednesday announced Chief Financial Officer Keith Taylor's intention to retire in 2026. 
  • Taylor, who joined Equinix in 1999, will remain as CFO until a successor is selected, and then as a Special Advisor to the company for approximately one year to ensure a smooth transition.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Essex prices $350M of senior notes
data.description:
• Essex (NYSE:ESS) announced  on Wednesday that its operating partnership, Essex Portfolio, L.P priced an underwritten public offering of $350M of 4.875% senior notes due 2036.
  • The notes were priced at 99.093% of par value with a yield to maturity of 4.988%.
  • Interest is payable semiannually at an interest rate per annum of 4.875% on February 15 and August 15 of each year, with the first interest payment due August 15, 2026. 
  • The offering is expected to close on December 12, 2025.
  • The issuer intends to use the net proceeds of this offering to repay the company’s $450M  outstanding amount of 3.375% senior notes due April 2026.
  • Furthermore, the company plans to use net proceeds for other general corporate and working capital purposes like funding potential acquisition opportunities.
  • Wells Fargo Securities, LLC, and J.P. Morgan Securities LLC served as joint book-running managers.
  • BMO Capital Markets Corp., Regions Securities LLC, Scotia Capital (USA) and Truist Securities, Inc. served as senior co-managers, and Samuel A. Ramirez & Company, Inc. served as co-manager for the offering.

data.title: Salesforce ticks up as Q4, full-year guidance impress bulls
data.description:
Salesforce (CRM) shares rose 4% in extended trading on Wednesday after the cloud software giant offered up guidance for the next quarter and fiscal 2026 that were above Wall Street's forecast. 

Looking to the fiscal fourth-quarter, the Marc Benioff-led company now expects revenue to be between $11.13B and $11.23B, with the midpoint above the $10.91B estimate. Adjusted earnings are forecast to be between $3.02 and $3.04 per share, in-line with the $3.03 per share estimate. Current remaining performance obligations are expected to grow 15% year-over-year, Salesforce added.

In addition, Salesforce upped its forecast for fiscal 2026, as it now sees revenue between $41.145B and $41.55B, above its previous range of $41.1B and $41.3B, and above the $41.25B analyst estimate. Salesforce also now expects to earn between $11.75 and $11.77 per share on an adjusted basis, above its previous forecast of $11.33 to $11.37 per share. Analysts were expecting $11.37 per share in adjusted earnings. 

The stronger-than-expected guidance comes as Salesforce reported mixed fiscal third-quarter results. For the period ending Oct. 31, Salesforce earned an adjusted $3.25 per share as revenue rose 8.6% year-over-year to $10.26B. Included in that was a 9.5% jump from subscription and support revenue to $9.73B, aided in part by Agentforce and Data 360. Salesforce also said that professional services and other revenue was $533M during the quarter, a decline of 5.7% year-over-year. 

Analysts expected the company to earn $2.86 per share on an adjusted basis, with $10.28B in revenue.

Remaining performance obligations rose 12% year-over-year to $59.5N, while current remaining performance obligation came in at $29.4B, up 11% year-over-year. 

“Our Agentforce and Data 360 products are the momentum drivers, hitting nearly $1.4 billion in ARR—an explosive 114% year-over-year gain,” Benioff said in a statement. “We now have over 9,500 paid Agentforce deals and 3.2 trillion tokens processed, underscoring our leadership in building the Agentic Enterprise and driving real outcomes.”

Seeking Alpha analyst Julia Ostian said the results “look great,” though the weak momentum from the company does not make her want to “rush” to buy the stock.

“I personally would still want to see how the market reacts in the next few weeks, because the perception around Salesforce has been bad the entire year, and with the stock down almost 25% YTD, it could also be a tax-harvesting candidate for many portfolios in December,” Ostian said via email.

The company will host a conference call at 5 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

(This story has been updated to include analyst commentary.)

data.title: Symbotic launches 10M share offering
data.description:
• Symbotic (SYM)said it has begun an underwritten public offering of 10 million Class A shares, including 6.5 million offered by the company and 3.5 million by SVF Sponsor III [DE] LLC, an affiliate of SoftBank Group.
  • The company plans to grant underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to 1.5 million additional shares.
  • Symbotic expects to use its share of proceeds for general corporate purposes, while the selling securityholder will receive proceeds from its portion of the sale.
  • SYM -12.83% after hours to $63.82.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Costco reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Costco press release (COST): Q1 Revenue of $65.98B (+6.2% Y/Y) misses by $1.06B.

  • Costco Wholesale Corporation  today reported net sales of $23.64 billion for the retail month of November, the four weeks ended November 30, 2025, an increase of 8.1 percent from $21.87 billion last year.

data.title: UiPath surges as Q3 results, guidance top expectations
data.description:
UiPath (PATH) shares surged more than 10% in extended trading on Wednesday after the software company reported third-quarter results and guidance that topped Wall Street's forecast. 

For the period ending Oct. 31, UiPath said it earned an adjusted $0.16 per share as revenue rose 15.9% year-over-year to $411.11M. Analysts had expected the company to earn an adjusted $0.15 per share on $392.8M.

Looking ahead, UiPath expects fourth-quarter revenue to be between $462M and $467M, with the $464.5M midpoint above the $463.1M estimate. Annualized recurring revenue is expected to be between $1.844B and $1.849B as of Jan. 31, 2026, the company added.

“I am pleased with our third quarter results delivering ARR of $1.782 billion, up 11 percent year-over-year, a testament to the team’s focus, consistent execution, and the momentum we're seeing as customers scale agentic automation across the enterprise,” said Daniel Dines, UiPath Founder and Chief Executive Officer. “Enterprises are accelerating their AI and automation strategies, and they’re looking for a unified platform rather than standalone tools. Our ability to bring deterministic automation, agentic automation, and orchestration together in one trusted, governed system is a true differentiator. It’s delivering meaningful outcomes for customers and positions us well as we close out the year.”

The company will host a conference call at 5 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Methode Electronics Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.19 beats by $0.01, revenue of $246.9M beats by $9.21M
data.description:
• Methode Electronics press release (MEI): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.19 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $246.9M (-15.6% Y/Y) beats by $9.21M.

  • For fiscal 2026, the company reaffirmed its guidance expectation as follows:

    Fiscal 2026 $ Millions
    Sales $900-1,000
    Interest Expense $21-23
    Tax Expense $17-21
    D&A $58-63
    Adjusted EBITDA $70-80
    Capital Expenditures $24-29
